summary: Parallel review: FAIL (1 critical, 7 warning, 14 suggestion). Compound gate: PASS

findingsList.text: 🟡 `bin/lib/util.mjs:37` — `getFlag` returns the next arg even if it's another flag (e.g., `--runbook --dry-run` returns `"--dry-run"` as the runbook value). Pre-existing issue, not introduced by this PR, but exposed by it. Consider adding a `startsWith("-")` guard.

🟡 `bin/lib/runbooks.mjs:244` — Keyword substring matching (`"api"` inside `"capital"`) is documented as intentional with clear rationale. Monitor for false positives; word-boundary matching is a natural evolution if needed.
🟡 `.team/runbooks/add-github-integration.yml:1` — Doesn't use `include: shared-setup` unlike `add-cli-command.yml`. Inconsistent composability pattern. File as future work.

🟡 bin/lib/util.mjs:36 — `getFlag` consumes next flag as value when `--runbook` is followed by another flag (e.g., `--runbook --dry-run`). Pre-existing shared pattern, not a regression — add to backlog for project-wide `getFlag` improvement to reject values starting with `--`.

🟡 test/runbooks.test.mjs — No test for YAML files with BOM (byte order mark). `readFileSync("utf8")` includes BOM in the first key, causing `id` validation to fail silently. Low risk since runbook files are author-controlled.
Previous 🟡 (prototype pollution) has been **resolved**. No critical or warning findings. Evaluation written to `.team/features/runbook-system/tasks/task-sec-final/eval.md`.
🟡 bin/lib/runbooks.mjs:18 — Custom YAML parser (72 lines) is the largest single-function complexity; if the runbook schema stays flat, consider JSON format to eliminate the parser entirely
**No 🔴 critical findings.** All four veto categories checked:
